ODM.Description: Veterans PTSD: Psilocybin Safety & Therapeutic Monitoring (Mock Study)

GlobalVariables.StudyName: Veterans PTSD: Psilocybin Safety & Therapeutic Monitoring (Mock Study)
GlobalVariables.StudyDescription: This file contains the metadata, events, and data for REDCap project "Veterans PTSD: Psilocybin Safety & Therapeutic Monitoring (Mock Study)".
GlobalVariables.ProtocolName: Veterans PTSD: Psilocybin Safety & Therapeutic Monitoring (Mock Study)

MetaDataVersion.Name: Veterans PTSD: Psilocybin Safety & Therapeutic Monitoring (Mock Study)

ItemGroupDef.Name: PTSD CHECKLIST FOR DSM-5 (PCL-5) - BASELINE Below are 20 statements about PTSD symptoms. Rate each on a scale from 0 (Not at all) to 4 (Extremely). Base your responses on your worst month of PTSD symptoms in the past month.
PTSD CHECKLIST FOR DSM-5 (PCL-5) - FOLLOW-UP ASSESSMENT

Question.TranslatedText: Subject ID
Date of Baseline Visit
Age (years)

ErrorMessage.TranslatedText: The value you provided is outside the suggested range (18 - 120). This value is admissible, but you may wish to double check it.
The value you provided is outside the suggested range (18 - 120). This value is admissible, but you may wish to double check it.

Question.TranslatedText: Military Service Branch
Years of Active Service

ErrorMessage.TranslatedText: The value you provided is outside the suggested range (0 - 60). This value is admissible, but you may wish to double check it.
The value you provided is outside the suggested range (0 - 60). This value is admissible, but you may wish to double check it.

Question.TranslatedText: When were you diagnosed with PTSD? (approximate year is fine)
History of Military Sexual Trauma (MST)?
Have you had any previous experience with psychedelic substances (psilocybin, LSD, mescaline, DMT)?
Systolic Blood Pressure (mm Hg)

ErrorMessage.TranslatedText: The value you provided is outside the suggested range (70 - 250). This value is admissible, but you may wish to double check it.
The value you provided is outside the suggested range (70 - 250). This value is admissible, but you may wish to double check it.

Question.TranslatedText: Diastolic Blood Pressure (mm Hg)

ErrorMessage.TranslatedText: The value you provided is outside the suggested range (40 - 160). This value is admissible, but you may wish to double check it.
The value you provided is outside the suggested range (40 - 160). This value is admissible, but you may wish to double check it.

Question.TranslatedText: Heart Rate (beats per minute)

ErrorMessage.TranslatedText: The value you provided is outside the suggested range (40 - 150). This value is admissible, but you may wish to double check it.
The value you provided is outside the suggested range (40 - 150). This value is admissible, but you may wish to double check it.

Question.TranslatedText: Body Temperature (°F)

ErrorMessage.TranslatedText: The value you provided is outside the suggested range (95.0 - 105.0). This value is admissible, but you may wish to double check it.
The value you provided is outside the suggested range (95.0 - 105.0). This value is admissible, but you may wish to double check it.

Question.TranslatedText: Current Psychiatric Medications (check all that apply)
Current Psychiatric Medications (check all that apply)
Current Psychiatric Medications (check all that apply)
Current Psychiatric Medications (check all that apply)
Current Psychiatric Medications (check all that apply)
Current Psychiatric Medications (check all that apply)
Current Psychiatric Medications (check all that apply)
Have you used alcohol or drugs in the last 30 days?
Are you currently having thoughts of harming yourself or ending your life?
Any medical conditions that could contraindicate psilocybin? (e.g., uncontrolled hypertension, cardiac issues, seizure disorder)
Medical Team Clearance: Subject cleared for psilocybin administration?

ItemDef.Name: pcl5_b1
ItemDef.redcap:SectionHeader: PTSD CHECKLIST FOR DSM-5 (PCL-5) - BASELINE Below are 20 statements about PTSD symptoms. Rate each on a scale from 0 (Not at all) to 4 (Extremely). Base your responses on your worst month of PTSD symptoms in the past month.

Question.TranslatedText: B1. Repeated, disturbing memories, thoughts, or images of the stressful experience?
B2. Repeated, disturbing dreams of the stressful experience?
B3. Sudden, frightening flashbacks of the stressful experience?
B4. Feeling very upset when reminded of the stressful experience?
B5. Having strong physical reactions when reminded of the stressful experience?
C1. Avoiding memories, thoughts, or feelings related to the stressful experience?
C2. Avoiding external reminders of the stressful experience (places, people, conversations, activities, objects, or situations)?
D1. Trouble remembering important parts of the stressful experience?
D2. Having strong negative beliefs about yourself, other people, or the world?
D3. Blaming yourself or someone else for the stressful experience or what happened after it?
D4. Having strong negative feelings such as fear, anger, guilt, or shame?
D5. Interest in activities has decreased?
D6. Feeling distant or cut off from other people?
D7. Difficulty experiencing positive emotions?
E1. Irritable or aggressive behavior?
E2. Taking too many risks or doing things that could cause you harm?
E3. Being "on guard" or watchful or alert?
E4. Your startle response (for example, being startled by a loud noise or surprise)?
E5. Difficulty concentrating?
E6. Trouble falling or staying asleep?
PCL-5 Total Score (Baseline)
Complete?
What time did the session begin?
Psilocybin dose administered (mg)

ErrorMessage.TranslatedText: The value you provided is outside the suggested range (0.5 - 50.0). This value is admissible, but you may wish to double check it.
The value you provided is outside the suggested range (0.5 - 50.0). This value is admissible, but you may wish to double check it.

Question.TranslatedText: BP Systolic at session start (mm Hg)

ErrorMessage.TranslatedText: The value you provided is outside the suggested range (70 - 250). This value is admissible, but you may wish to double check it.
The value you provided is outside the suggested range (70 - 250). This value is admissible, but you may wish to double check it.

Question.TranslatedText: BP Diastolic at session start (mm Hg)

ErrorMessage.TranslatedText: The value you provided is outside the suggested range (40 - 160). This value is admissible, but you may wish to double check it.
The value you provided is outside the suggested range (40 - 160). This value is admissible, but you may wish to double check it.

Question.TranslatedText: Heart Rate at session start (bpm)

ErrorMessage.TranslatedText: The value you provided is outside the suggested range (40 - 150). This value is admissible, but you may wish to double check it.
The value you provided is outside the suggested range (40 - 150). This value is admissible, but you may wish to double check it.

Question.TranslatedText: Did a serious adverse event occur during this session?
Type of adverse event:
Severity of adverse event:
Describe the adverse event and interventions taken:
Was pharmacological or other intervention given?
What intervention was given? (medication, technique, etc.)
Was session completed, or was psilocybin terminated early?
Complete?
How many days have passed since psilocybin session?

ErrorMessage.TranslatedText: The value you provided is outside the suggested range (0 - 180). This value is admissible, but you may wish to double check it.
The value you provided is outside the suggested range (0 - 180). This value is admissible, but you may wish to double check it.

Question.TranslatedText: Has a serious adverse event occurred since we last spoke?
Type of adverse event:
Date of adverse event (D-M-Y)
Has this adverse event resolved?
Current Systolic Blood Pressure (mm Hg):

ErrorMessage.TranslatedText: The value you provided is outside the suggested range (70 - 250). This value is admissible, but you may wish to double check it.
The value you provided is outside the suggested range (70 - 250). This value is admissible, but you may wish to double check it.

Question.TranslatedText: Current Diastolic Blood Pressure (mm Hg):

ErrorMessage.TranslatedText: The value you provided is outside the suggested range (40 - 160). This value is admissible, but you may wish to double check it.
The value you provided is outside the suggested range (40 - 160). This value is admissible, but you may wish to double check it.

Question.TranslatedText: Current Heart Rate (bpm):

ErrorMessage.TranslatedText: The value you provided is outside the suggested range (40 - 150). This value is admissible, but you may wish to double check it.
The value you provided is outside the suggested range (40 - 150). This value is admissible, but you may wish to double check it.

Question.TranslatedText: Have you returned to your normal daily activities and work?
Is it safe for you to drive?
Have you used alcohol or any recreational drugs since the session?
What substance and when?
Are you currently having thoughts of harming yourself?
Please describe your thoughts and any plan:
Complete?

ItemDef.Name: pcl5_fu_b1
ItemDef.redcap:SectionHeader: PTSD CHECKLIST FOR DSM-5 (PCL-5) - FOLLOW-UP ASSESSMENT

Question.TranslatedText: B1. Repeated, disturbing memories, thoughts, or images of the stressful experience?
B2. Repeated, disturbing dreams of the stressful experience?
B3. Sudden, frightening flashbacks of the stressful experience?
B4. Feeling very upset when reminded of the stressful experience?
B5. Having strong physical reactions when reminded of the stressful experience?
C1. Avoiding memories, thoughts, or feelings related to the stressful experience?
C2. Avoiding external reminders of the stressful experience (places, people, conversations, activities, objects, or situations)?
D1. Trouble remembering important parts of the stressful experience?
D2. Having strong negative beliefs about yourself, other people, or the world?
D3. Blaming yourself or someone else for the stressful experience or what happened after it?
D4. Having strong negative feelings such as fear, anger, guilt, or shame?
D5. Interest in activities has decreased?
D6. Feeling distant or cut off from other people?
D7. Difficulty experiencing positive emotions?
E1. Irritable or aggressive behavior?
E2. Taking too many risks or doing things that could cause you harm?
E3. Being "on guard" or watchful or alert?
E4. Your startle response (for example, being startled by a loud noise or surprise)?
E5. Difficulty concentrating?
E6. Trouble falling or staying asleep?
PCL-5 Total Score (Follow-Up)
Over the past two weeks, how often have you been bothered by little interest or pleasure in doing things?
Over the past two weeks, how often have you been bothered by feeling down, depressed, or hopeless?
Compared to before the psilocybin session, how would you rate your overall improvement?
Please describe any benefits or improvements you've noticed:
Complete?

CodeList.Name: current_psych_meds___1
CodeList.redcap:CheckboxChoices: 1, SSRI (Sertraline, Escitalopram, etc.) | 2, SNRI (Venlafaxine, Duloxetine, etc.) | 3, Atypical Antipsychotic (Aripiprazole, Quetiapine, etc.) | 4, Benzodiazepine (Alprazolam, Clonazepam, etc.) | 5, Mood stabilizer (Lithium, Valproate, etc.) | 6, None | 7, Other

CodeList.Name: current_psych_meds___2
CodeList.redcap:CheckboxChoices: 1, SSRI (Sertraline, Escitalopram, etc.) | 2, SNRI (Venlafaxine, Duloxetine, etc.) | 3, Atypical Antipsychotic (Aripiprazole, Quetiapine, etc.) | 4, Benzodiazepine (Alprazolam, Clonazepam, etc.) | 5, Mood stabilizer (Lithium, Valproate, etc.) | 6, None | 7, Other

CodeList.Name: current_psych_meds___3
CodeList.redcap:CheckboxChoices: 1, SSRI (Sertraline, Escitalopram, etc.) | 2, SNRI (Venlafaxine, Duloxetine, etc.) | 3, Atypical Antipsychotic (Aripiprazole, Quetiapine, etc.) | 4, Benzodiazepine (Alprazolam, Clonazepam, etc.) | 5, Mood stabilizer (Lithium, Valproate, etc.) | 6, None | 7, Other

CodeList.Name: current_psych_meds___4
CodeList.redcap:CheckboxChoices: 1, SSRI (Sertraline, Escitalopram, etc.) | 2, SNRI (Venlafaxine, Duloxetine, etc.) | 3, Atypical Antipsychotic (Aripiprazole, Quetiapine, etc.) | 4, Benzodiazepine (Alprazolam, Clonazepam, etc.) | 5, Mood stabilizer (Lithium, Valproate, etc.) | 6, None | 7, Other

CodeList.Name: current_psych_meds___5
CodeList.redcap:CheckboxChoices: 1, SSRI (Sertraline, Escitalopram, etc.) | 2, SNRI (Venlafaxine, Duloxetine, etc.) | 3, Atypical Antipsychotic (Aripiprazole, Quetiapine, etc.) | 4, Benzodiazepine (Alprazolam, Clonazepam, etc.) | 5, Mood stabilizer (Lithium, Valproate, etc.) | 6, None | 7, Other

CodeList.Name: current_psych_meds___6
CodeList.redcap:CheckboxChoices: 1, SSRI (Sertraline, Escitalopram, etc.) | 2, SNRI (Venlafaxine, Duloxetine, etc.) | 3, Atypical Antipsychotic (Aripiprazole, Quetiapine, etc.) | 4, Benzodiazepine (Alprazolam, Clonazepam, etc.) | 5, Mood stabilizer (Lithium, Valproate, etc.) | 6, None | 7, Other

CodeList.Name: current_psych_meds___7
CodeList.redcap:CheckboxChoices: 1, SSRI (Sertraline, Escitalopram, etc.) | 2, SNRI (Venlafaxine, Duloxetine, etc.) | 3, Atypical Antipsychotic (Aripiprazole, Quetiapine, etc.) | 4, Benzodiazepine (Alprazolam, Clonazepam, etc.) | 5, Mood stabilizer (Lithium, Valproate, etc.) | 6, None | 7, Other

Decode.TranslatedText: Psilocybin terminated early due to safety

ItemData.Value: I still feel the effects, everything feels calm but also a bit foggy. I'm definitely less anxious than usual and I slept better last night than I have in months, and I didn't have any nightmares.

I've noticed some real changes. The constant buzzing in my head is quieter. My nightmares are less frequent, maybe 2 nights instead of every night. I'm still processing a lot but in a different way and I'm less angry overall.

I've seen significant improvement. Yesterday, I went to my nephew's birthday party, the first family event I've been able to handle in years. I still have some triggers but they don't hijack me like before. Although it's moderately hard for me to fall asleep at a reasonable time, I do sleep through most nights now. My therapist says I'm more open in sessions. It feels like I'm starting to live again.

This has been life-changing. My insomnia has improved and the nightmares are almost gone. I can drive on highways without gripping the steering wheel. I even started going to the VA gym with other veterans, which is something I couldn't do before. My marriage feels stronger because I no longer have outbursts. I still have hard days but they're way more manageable now. Most of all, I feel like I got my sense of humor back. This is the first time in 8 years I feel like there's hope.
